headline: Opus-4.8 solves the PDE solver perfectly (7/7) and Kimi-K2.6 nearly so (6/7, 99%), but the field then splits violently — case pass ranges from 93% down to 44% as weaker models' solvers crash with exit code 1.
difficulty:
This is a brownfield scientific task: extend the existing numsim library with full sparse-matrix support, iterative Krylov solvers (CG, GMRES) with preconditioning, and a 2D Poisson solver that exercises all of it, across 7 rounds. The agent must respect numsim's existing matrix and history-output contracts rather than write fresh code.

The hardest commonly-failed requirements are poisson-mixed-bc (9/10 models) and ssor-omega-validation (7/10) — mixed-boundary Poisson and the SSOR omega-parameter validation. The history/reporting contracts also bite many: history-format, history-precond-col and convdiff-history (6 each), where the iteration log must emit the exact tab-separated columns. For the weaker models the failures are not subtle formatting misses but outright crashes — heat-backward-euler, convdiff-solver and cg-solver cases return "exit code 1" where the reference produces a converged solution, meaning the solver itself is broken.
gap:
The top tier is genuinely excellent. Opus-4.8 passes 7/7 rounds at 100%, and Kimi-K2.6 takes 6/7 at 99%, losing only a single poisson-mixed-bc case. Just below, GPT-5.5 and GLM-5.1 each clear 2/7 (83% / 84%) but lose the eigenvalue suite — eig-power, eigenvector-output, eig-shifted-inverse all returning "exit code 1" — and DeepSeek-V4-Pro lands 1/7 at a high 93%, missing mostly history-format and convdiff-solver.

Below them the implementations fall apart structurally. Qwen3.6-Plus is the anomaly: 0/7 rounds but 97% case pass, a nearly-complete solver undone only by ssor-omega-validation (6) and precond-ssor (2). By contrast the genuinely weak models crash on core solvers — MiniMax-M3 (61%), MiniMax-M2.7 (70%) and DeepSeek-V4-Flash (73%) sink dozens of cases into heat-backward-euler and convdiff-solver, and Qwen3.7-Max (44%) fails even the basics, matvec (22) and cg-solver (15). The separator here is whether the iterative solvers run at all, not whether the reporting is pixel-perfect.
